title: Argument handling, SkillTool integration, and resource listing
purpose: The SkillTool needs to accept arguments, parse them with shell-style quoting, map named arguments from frontmatter, apply the $ARGUMENTS auto-append, and include the skill directory and bundled resource listing in the activation result. This brief modifies the existing tools/skill.rs to use the new skill module infrastructure.
task: Modify tools/skill.rs to add an optional arguments parameter. Implement shell-style argument parsing (double-quoted, single-quoted, backslash escapes). After loading and parsing the skill, call skill/template.rs expansion with the parsed arguments. Implement $ARGUMENTS auto-append. Include skill directory path and bundled resource listing (capped at 20 entries) in the tool result.

requirements.title: Arguments parameter on SkillTool
requirements.spec: The SkillTool input schema SHALL accept an optional 'arguments' field (string type).
acceptance: SkillTool input_schema() includes an 'arguments' field with type 'string'
The field is not in the 'required' array

requirements.title: Shell-style argument parsing
requirements.spec: Arguments SHALL be parsed using shell-style quoting: double-quoted and single-quoted strings are single arguments (quotes stripped), unquoted words split on whitespace, backslash escapes within quotes.
acceptance: Empty string parses to empty vec

requirements.title: Named argument mapping
requirements.spec: WHEN the skill's SkillMetadata has a non-empty arguments list, positional arguments SHALL be mapped to named arguments in order.
acceptance: With arguments=['issue','branch'] and args ['123','main'], named map contains issue='123' and branch='main'
With more names than args, excess names map to empty string
With more args than names, excess args accessible only by $N

requirements.title: $ARGUMENTS auto-append
requirements.spec: IF $ARGUMENTS does not appear in the skill body (before expansion), the raw arguments string SHALL be appended as 'ARGUMENTS: <value>' after expansion.
acceptance: A skill body containing '$ARGUMENTS' does NOT get the auto-append
A skill body without '$ARGUMENTS', invoked with arguments='hello', has '\nARGUMENTS: hello' appended
A skill body without '$ARGUMENTS', invoked without arguments, does NOT get the auto-append

requirements.title: SkillTool uses skill module and includes resource listing
requirements.spec: The SkillTool execute method SHALL use skill/loader.rs for loading, skill/template.rs for expansion, strip frontmatter, and include the skill directory path and bundled resource listing (capped at 20 entries) in the JSON result.
acceptance: SkillTool imports from crate::skill::loader and crate::skill::template
Returned JSON includes name, path, content, skill_dir, and resources fields
resources lists files in the skill directory (excluding SKILL.md), capped at 20

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement slash command registration — that is NS-006
SHALL NOT implement effort override — that is NS-005
SHALL NOT implement the SkillCatalog query — that is NS-007
verification: cargo test -p norn -- tools::skill passes all tests (new and existing)
Existing skill tool tests continue to pass (backward compatible for basic load-by-name)
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
